name: Normalized artifact storage
statement: All provenance artifacts (sources, requirements, resolutions, rules) shall be stored in separate normalized tables with typed graph edges, not embedded sub-documents

name: Recursive requirement hierarchy
statement: Requirements shall compose recursively via parentId to arbitrary depth without artificial tier limits. No separate initiative/epic/story types.

name: One-directional broker sync
statement: Rule definitions shall sync one-directionally from Provenance to the Statesman broker. The broker owns contract-to-rule linkage and coverage tracking. Provenance shall not consume broker state.

name: Bidirectional traceability
statement: The system shall support both forward traceability (source -> requirements -> resolutions -> rules) and backward traceability (rule -> resolution -> requirement -> source) as first-class query operations

name: Human-attributable writes
statement: All writes to provenance artifacts must be attributable to a human identity (seat owner). AI-authored content shall be marked via message role=assistant while retaining the owning human as the author.

name: ELK layout with zone partitioning
statement: The canvas UI shall use ELK for automatic graph layout with zone partitioning that groups nodes by artifact type into distinct visual regions

name: Annotation vocabulary mirrors contract metadata
statement: External test annotations shall use the same metadata fields as Statesman contracts: name, description, tags (from Contract trait), rule code, coverage_level, confidence (from RuleContract). No new annotation-specific concepts.

name: No project management labels
statement: Annotations shall not include epic, feature, or story labels. Statesman is not a project management tool. The requirement tree provides hierarchy; Provenance derives structural context from the rule's graph chain.

name: Comment-based universal baseline
statement: A comment-based annotation format shall serve as the zero-dependency universal baseline that works in any programming language. Per-language native APIs (decorators, attributes, annotations) are optional ergonomic sugar that serialize to the same output format.

name: Scanner lives in Statesman CLI
statement: The static analysis scanner shall be built into the Statesman CLI binary, not as a standalone tool. This gives external tests access to traceability, impact analysis, gap detection, audit trails, and CI integration for free.

name: Scan-time stale annotation validation
statement: The scanner shall cross-reference extracted annotations against the project's active rules and warn on unknown rule codes, deprecated rules, and archived rules to prevent false coverage confidence.

name: Top-level coverage commands
statement: Coverage commands shall live at 'statesman coverage', not nested under 'statesman provenance'. Coverage spans both contract coverage from the broker and external test evidence from annotations.

name: Thread diffs on definitive changes only
statement: When coverage changes are pushed from a scan (not a dry-run), diffs shall be posted to affected rule threads in Provenance: new coverage added, coverage removed, confidence or level changed. Dry-runs shall not post to threads.

name: External coverage stored in broker
statement: External test coverage evidence shall be stored in the broker via RuleContract with mapping_source=imported. The broker is the single source of truth for all coverage data. Provenance pulls coverage from the broker unchanged.

name: Runtime fields optional on coverage API
statement: The broker coverage API and annotation format shall not require runtime fields (pass/fail result, timestamp, duration, pass rate). These fields must be optional extensions that future runtime reporters can provide without breaking the static-analysis-only path.

name: Simple key-value comment syntax
statement: Annotations shall use @statesman key: value syntax inside line comments. No function-call syntax, no parentheses, no quotes. Comments should look like comments, not code.

name: Rule field is the only required annotation
statement: The @statesman rule: field shall be the only required annotation field. All other fields (name, description, tags, coverage, confidence, intent) are optional with sensible defaults: name inferred from function, coverage defaults to full, confidence defaults to 1.0.

name: Function-scoped by default with file-level override
statement: Annotations immediately above a test function shall apply to that test only (function-scoped). Annotations at the top of a file before any function shall apply to all tests in that file (file-scoped). Function-level annotations override file-level for the same field.

name: Multi-rule via separate rule lines
statement: A single test may cover multiple rules using one @statesman rule: line per rule. Coverage and confidence following a rule line apply to that rule. Coverage and confidence preceding all rules or standing alone apply to all rules in the block.

name: Comment prefix detected from file extension
statement: The scanner shall detect the line comment prefix from file extension: // for Rust/C/Java/Go/JS/TS/C#/Swift/Kotlin, # for Python/Ruby/Shell/YAML/TOML, -- for SQL/Lua/Haskell, % for Erlang/LaTeX. Block comments are not supported in v1.

name: Line-oriented parser only
statement: The scanner shall be a line-oriented text parser with comment-prefix awareness. It shall not parse ASTs, resolve imports, execute tests, or modify source code.

name: Tree-sitter AST-based scanning
statement: The scanner shall use tree-sitter for AST parsing with statically linked grammars. Comment-to-code association, function detection, and block boundaries shall be derived from the AST, not line-oriented regex heuristics. This supersedes PROV-ANNOT-015.

name: JSON scanner output format
statement: The scanner shall produce JSON output containing: project_id, scanned_at timestamp, files_scanned count, tests_found count, an annotations array (file, line, function, name, description, tags, rules with code/coverage/confidence), and a warnings array for stale or unknown annotations.

name: Full scan every run in v1
statement: The scanner shall perform a full scan of all matching files on every invocation in v1. Incremental scanning (content hashing, git-based change detection) is a future optimization, not a v1 requirement.

name: External test evidence as first-class records
statement: External tests shall be stored as evidence records in the broker containing test metadata (file, line, function, name, description, tags). RuleContract shall link to evidence records the same way it links to contract versions. No nullable FKs.

name: Bulk evidence import endpoint
statement: The broker shall expose a bulk import endpoint for external test evidence that accepts the scanner's JSON output and creates/updates evidence records and rule linkages in a single request. One-at-a-time creation via existing link_contract is insufficient for CI pipelines scanning hundreds of tests.

name: Four coverage subcommands
statement: The statesman coverage command shall have four subcommands: 'scan' (scan + push in one shot), 'scan --dry-run' (scan only, output JSON, do not push or post threads), 'report' (accept pre-computed scan JSON from stdin and push to broker), and 'status' (show current coverage summary from broker).

name: WorkOS role and permissions in JWT claims
statement: The control plane must extract role and permissions from WorkOS access tokens and include them in Statesman JWT ClaimsProperties so downstream services (Provenance, broker) can read them without additional API calls.

name: Per-mutation permission enforcement in Provenance
statement: Every Provenance mutation must call requirePermission() with the appropriate resource:action permission string. The check must be backward-compatible: coarse read/write scopes grant all reads or all writes respectively, and admin grants everything.

name: Agent tokens use sm_agent_ prefix and carry identity_type
statement: Agent tokens must use the sm_agent_ prefix to distinguish them from human API keys (sm_live_). When exchanged for a JWT, the claims must include identity_type=agent, agent_id, and owner_id fields.

name: Agent tokens must have mandatory expiration
statement: Agent tokens must have a non-null expires_at timestamp. The system must provide a token rotation command. Expired tokens must be rejected at exchange time.

name: Agent tokens inherit project scope from owner by default
statement: When agent_tokens.project_ids is NULL, the agent inherits its owner's project access. Explicit project_ids must be a subset of the owner's access.

name: Default roles exclude resolutions:approve for agents
statement: The system default role sets (read-only, contributor, analyst) must not include the resolutions:approve permission. Only full-access and custom organization roles may grant approval rights.

name: Delegation tokens must narrow permissions, never widen
statement: When an agent creates a delegation token for another agent, the delegated permissions must be a strict subset of the delegating agent's permissions. The human owner remains the same. The delegation chain must be traceable via created_by_agent_id.

name: Agents attributed via synthetic Convex user records
statement: Agents must have synthetic user records in the Convex users table with isAgent=true and ownerUserId linking to the human owner. This preserves all existing createdBy queries and UI rendering while enabling agent-aware display.

name: CLI agent output uses HAL+JSON with navigable _links
statement: All Provenance CLI commands that output JSON shall include HAL-style _links on each artifact, providing commands for available operations (self, traceability, graph, related artifacts). Agents consuming CLI output shall be able to discover and follow graph relationships without prior knowledge of available commands. Raw Convex JSON without _links shall be available via --raw-json for debugging.

name: Idempotent ideation job writes
statement: Ideation job writes shall use deterministic idempotency keys for participant comments, synthesis, proposal extraction, and artifact links so retries or duplicate workers do not create duplicate Provenance records.

name: Visible partial ideation failures
statement: If any persona participant, model call, synthesis step, or thread post fails, the ideation run shall expose partial status, failed step identity, retry state, and diagnostic context rather than presenting the run as fully successful.

name: Human promotion gate for ideation outputs
statement: Persona-generated ideation comments, synthesis, and proposal cards shall not become canonical requirements, resolutions, rules, or work items unless a human explicitly accepts or edits the proposal, with the promoted artifact linked back to the originating thread messages and run metadata.

name: Bounded artifact-attached ideation threads
statement: Each multi-persona ideation run shall target exactly one primary Provenance artifact or problem and shall record bounded participant comments plus a synthesis in an artifact-attached thread.

name: Persona profile attribution
statement: Every persona participant output shall record the persona/profile name, profile version, prompt/template version, executing agent identity, model/config metadata, timestamp, target artifact, and owning human or service attribution.

name: Dissent and evidence preservation
statement: Ideation synthesis shall explicitly preserve consensus, contested claims, minority objections, evidence strength, unsupported speculation, source gaps, and open assumptions; it shall not collapse disagreement into unqualified consensus.

name: Stable ideation run correlation ID
statement: Every ideation run SHALL mint a stable ideation_run_id that is recorded on the work item, participant executions, thread comments, synthesis, proposal cards, retries, failures, and any promoted artifacts.

name: Ideation run status lifecycle
statement: Ideation runs SHALL expose an explicit status lifecycle: queued, running, synthesizing, succeeded, partial, failed_retryable, failed_terminal, and cancelled. A run with any failed participant or failed post SHALL NOT be reported as succeeded.

name: Ideation input snapshot
statement: Each ideation run SHALL record an input snapshot containing target artifact type/id, selected source IDs, graph scope/hash or timestamp, existing thread IDs considered, persona profile versions, prompt/template versions, model identifiers, and configured budget limits.

name: Bounded participant and round limits
statement: An ideation run SHALL enforce configured maximums for participant count, rounds, model calls, tokens, wall-clock duration, and generated bytes before execution starts; exceeding any limit SHALL cancel or partially fail the run visibly.

name: No unsupported authoritative persona claims
statement: Persona participants SHALL NOT state unsupported recommendations as authoritative product truth. If a participant recommends action without source or graph support, the output SHALL explicitly mark the recommendation as unsupported or exploratory.

name: Participant contribution schema
statement: Each persona participant comment SHALL include structured sections for stance, strongest finding, evidence references, risks, objections, suggested artifact changes, uncertainty, and open questions.

name: Machine-readable run inspection
statement: The system SHALL provide a machine-readable inspection view for an ideation run that returns run status, target artifact, input snapshot, participant statuses, message IDs, synthesis ID, proposal IDs, retry attempts, failure diagnostics, and promotion decisions.

name: Evidence labels on participant claims
statement: Participant comments SHALL label each material claim as source-backed, graph-backed, code-backed, prior-thread-backed, inference, or speculation. Speculative claims SHALL NOT be presented as findings in synthesis without the speculation label.

name: Deterministic participant slots
statement: Each participant output SHALL be keyed by (ideation_run_id, round_number, participant_slot, persona_profile_version). Retrying the same slot SHALL update or reuse the same logical output and SHALL NOT create an additional participant comment.

name: Challenge comments reference specific claims
statement: When a persona challenges another persona, the challenge SHALL reference the specific participant comment, claim, or proposal being challenged and SHALL state the reason for disagreement; generic dissent without a target is invalid.

name: Synthesis packet required sections
statement: Each ideation synthesis SHALL include sections for summary, consensus, contested claims, minority objections, evidence gaps, unsupported speculation, open questions, suggested artifacts, and required human decisions.

name: Typed ideation proposal cards
statement: Ideation synthesis SHALL emit proposal cards typed as one of: requirement_candidate, resolution_candidate, rule_candidate, work_item_candidate, source_gap, question, or no_action. Each proposal card SHALL declare exactly one type.

name: Duplicate proposal detection
statement: Before presenting proposal cards, the synthesis step SHALL detect duplicates within the same run by comparing proposal type, normalized proposed text, target artifact, and source evidence; duplicates SHALL be merged or marked duplicate.

name: Proposal promotion state lifecycle
statement: Proposal cards SHALL expose a promotion state lifecycle: proposed, accepted, rejected, deferred, duplicate, superseded. Only accepted proposals MAY create or update canonical Provenance artifacts.

name: Outcome-first ideation thread layout
statement: The default thread presentation for an ideation run SHALL show synthesis, proposal cards, contested claims, open questions, and visible failures before raw participant transcript content.

name: Synthesis readability budget
statement: Ideation synthesis SHALL be concise enough to serve as a review brief: it SHALL include no more than seven top-level findings unless explicitly expanded, and each proposal card SHALL be independently scannable without reading the transcript.

name: Persona label distinct from executor identity
statement: Ideation UI and API output SHALL display persona/profile labels separately from authenticated executor identity and owner attribution, so readers can distinguish the simulated perspective from the agent or human account that performed the write.

name: Raw transcript retained but collapsed
statement: Raw persona outputs and model transcripts SHALL be retained for audit when available, but SHALL be collapsed or secondary in the default UI so users are not required to read full transcripts to identify outcomes.

name: Proposal card traceability fields
statement: Each proposal card SHALL record origin participant message IDs, supporting claim IDs or excerpts, evidence level, dissent status, confidence, target artifact if applicable, proposed text, and promotion state.

name: Promoted artifact backlink requirement
statement: When a proposal card is accepted and promoted, the created or updated artifact SHALL link back to the ideation_run_id, synthesis message, origin participant comments, accepted proposal card, and human approval event.

name: Ideation project and target scope isolation
statement: An ideation run SHALL only read and write artifacts within the target artifact's project and configured scope. It SHALL reject participant prompts, tool calls, or proposal promotions that target artifacts outside the authorized project/scope.

name: Concurrent participant posting acceptance test
statement: A validation test SHALL run at least five participant posts concurrently against one target artifact and assert that the result contains one active thread, exactly one participant comment per participant slot, one synthesis record, and no duplicate threads or comments.

name: Partial failure acceptance test
statement: A validation test SHALL force one participant failure and assert that the ideation run status is partial or failed_retryable, successful participant outputs remain visible, failed slot diagnostics are inspectable, and synthesis does not claim a complete panel.

name: Participant retry idempotency acceptance test
statement: A validation test SHALL simulate a participant post succeeding followed by a worker crash before completion marking, then retry the same participant slot and assert that no duplicate comment or proposal is created and the existing output is associated with the retry attempt.

name: Human promotion traceability acceptance test
statement: A validation test SHALL accept a proposal card into a canonical artifact and assert that the artifact links to the ideation run, proposal card, synthesis, supporting participant comments, source evidence, and human approval event.

name: Unsafe ideation output handling
statement: If participant output is unsafe, policy-violating, or unsuitable for thread posting, the run SHALL record a sanitized failure marker with participant slot, reason category, and retry state; unsafe content SHALL NOT be posted as normal artifact discussion.

name: Source access and redaction enforcement
statement: Ideation runs SHALL respect source access policy and redaction rules. If a participant lacks access to a source, the run SHALL omit that source from the participant input snapshot and SHALL NOT leak its contents in comments or synthesis.

name: Ideation cancellation and deadline handling
statement: Ideation runs SHALL support cancellation and hard deadlines. A cancelled or deadline-expired run SHALL stop scheduling new participant work, preserve completed outputs, mark unfinished participant slots as cancelled or timed_out, and post or expose partial status.

name: One active ideation thread per parent under concurrency
statement: Concurrent ideation posting for the same parent artifact SHALL result in exactly one active thread for that parent. The implementation SHALL prevent or reconcile duplicate active threads created by concurrent find-or-create operations.

name: Ideation rate and budget limits
statement: The system SHALL enforce per-project and per-run limits for concurrent ideation runs, participant count, token spend, model call count, and thread writes. Limit exhaustion SHALL produce a visible bounded failure rather than silently skipping participants.

name: Run inspection conformance acceptance test
statement: A validation test SHALL call the ideation run inspection surface after success, partial failure, and cancellation and assert that all required fields from PROV-IDEATE-011 are present and internally consistent.

name: Ideation output schema validation
statement: Before an ideation run is marked succeeded, the implementation SHALL validate participant comments, synthesis, and proposal cards against the required schemas; missing required sections or malformed proposal cards SHALL produce partial or failed_retryable status.
